response:
{
  "discovered_categories": [
    {
      "name": "Surface Modification Characteristics",
      "description": "Physical and chemical properties of implant surfaces including roughness and hydrophilicity.",
      "examples_found": [
        "rough-hydrophilic surfaces",
        "Sa=3.33 um (rough Ti)",
        "contact angle 0deg (rough-hydrophilic)",
        "smooth Sa=0.6 um",
        "contact angle 86deg (rough), 62deg (smooth)"
      ]
    },
    {
      "name": "Immune Cell Polarization Responses",
      "description": "Polarization states and population changes of macrophages, T cells, neutrophils, and MSCs in response to different surfaces.",
      "examples_found": [
        "M2-like phenotype: higher arginase, IL-10 secretion",
        "M1-like phenotype: high IL-1beta, IL-6, TNFalpha",
        "rough-hydrophilic implants increased CD4+ and CD8+ T cells",
        "rough-hydrophilic reduced neutrophil presence",
        "obesity shifted toward pro-inflammatory phenotypes with higher CD8+ T cells, lower Th2 and Th17",
        "M1 macrophage transfer increased inflammatory cells",
        "macrophage ablation increased neutrophils, reduced T cells and MSCs"
      ]
    },
    {
      "name": "Tissue Healing and Structural Outcomes",
      "description": "Quantitative and qualitative measures of osseointegration and bone formation.",
      "examples_found": [
        "increased bone-to-implant contact with M2 transfer",
        "lowest osseointegration with M1 transfer",
        "compromised bone formation after macrophage ablation",
        "reduced MSC recruitment in obese mice"
      ]
    },
    {
      "name": "Experimental Models and Quantitative Data",
      "description": "Animal models, interventions, and quantitative measurements used.",
      "examples_found": [
        "C57BL/6J mice",
        "obese mice",
        "adoptive transfer of 1x10^6 macrophages/mouse",
        "macrophage ablation",
        "kidney injury model (M1 worsened injury)",
        "fracture healing model (M1 required for early healing)",
        "roughness Sa values: 3.33, 3.31, 0.6 um",
        "contact angles: 86deg, 0deg, 62deg"
      ]
    }
  ],
  "key_variables": [
    "surface roughness (Sa)",
    "surface hydrophilicity (contact angle)",
    "macrophage polarization (M1 vs M2)",
    "T cell subsets (CD4+, CD8+, Th2, Th17)",
    "neutrophil presence",
    "mesenchymal stem cell (MSC) recruitment",
    "cytokine levels (IL-10, IL-1beta, IL-6, TNFalpha)",
    "bone-to-implant contact",
    "osseointegration",
    "bone formation",
    "obesity status",
    "adoptive transfer cell type",
    "macrophage ablation"
  ],
  "experimental_methods": [
    "surface roughness measurement (profilometry)",
    "contact angle goniometry",
    "flow cytometry for immune cell phenotyping",
    "cytokine quantification (ELISA/multiplex)",
    "adoptive macrophage transfer",
    "macrophage ablation",
    "histomorphometry for bone-implant contact",
    "mouse models: C57BL/6J, obese (diet-induced)",
    "experimental injury models: kidney injury, fracture healing"
  ]
}
